lane: Sam 3 — effect broker / Git cell / real rehearsal
ownership: apps/seed/** only; never merge main; report on issue #22
status: MISSION SUBSTANTIVELY COMPLETE — the 10 code bricks fully specify the crash-recoverable effect protocol + its recovery table; 1 integration-map doc consolidates them. The single remaining brick (concrete EffectOps wiring into the live ceremony) was not startable because the base never bumped.
durabilityClaim: NONE end-to-end. Sam 2's durable trusted-state/PREPARED contract is in the comprehensive integration (#135), not on main, so every durable seam here is an injected interface + test double by design.
mission: Turn the direct ceremony-to-Git path into an explicit crash-recoverable effect protocol (PROPOSED->QUALIFIED->POLICY_REHEARSAL_PASSED->HERMETIC_REHEARSAL_PASSED->AWAITING_OWNER->PREPARED->EXECUTING->OBSERVED->COMMITTED + REFUSED/REHEARSAL_FAILED/CANCELLED_BEFORE_PREPARE/RECONCILE_REQUIRED/QUARANTINED/COMPENSATED), harden the Git isolation cell, and add the hostile-test matrix.

bricks.head: 07fbc1154184cff8d3cf3454195fde10fffd0d00
bricks.summary: HARDEN GIT — exact trusted binary (system dirs only), minimal env (empty HOME/XDG, system+global config off, hooksPath=/dev/null, no terminal prompt, file-protocol only), argv-only.
bricks.tests: seed 343 (+5 hostile: fake git in PATH / malicious hook / hostile GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL / hostile HOME / all-at-once)

bricks.head: 74283b360b00911434ef700198e7830d455dea3a
bricks.summary: The pure crash-recoverable state machine: advance()/reconcile(); 15 first-class phases; four invariants (git only after consuming PREPARED; observe-not-re-execute; no null-completion COMMITTED; no second candidate). PreparedEffectStore interface + InMemory double.

bricks.head: 441aff5b20eec9f7ea63cd96980a8705c7c63977
bricks.summary: Map-only policy/binding simulation (executes nothing) distinct from a hermetic rehearsal (fixed digest-bound plan in a no-secret/no-network cell, or honest unavailable). A caller names an approved plan id — never a shell command.

bricks.head: d24d73058a9f361461c3e6242d1f521732b32c27
bricks.summary: Protected-ref + tree isolation snapshots before/after; verifyIsolation is byte-exact fail-closed; any moved/added/removed ref or tree change quarantines.

bricks.head: 4c591486f908748830a1f54c9998e43d979cea06
bricks.summary: Post-effect settlement is projection-only: validateSettlement is total + fail-closed, refuses any authority-shaped key / extra key / content / a COMMITTED without a durable completion reference.

bricks.head: f54acc8fcfb4ab9bde70958ae5c4552695c55b7e
bricks.summary: Candidate path collision integrity — APFS-equivalence key (NFC + case fold) detects Unicode-normalization AND case-fold collisions the existing case-fold-only check missed.

bricks.head: 68aa9e85dc416be87fb38ce9627d5bfa2af12469
bricks.summary: Append-only content-free audit ledger enforcing the closing invariant over a whole effect life: no second EXECUTING per effect; no COMMITTED without a completion bit; verifyAuditLog re-proves both over a rehydrated log.

bricks.head: cde287c7b1f4fa53dedc700b31e1eab01ac8900d
bricks.summary: Git config threat classifier — detects command-executing/redirecting directives (filter/textconv drivers, hooksPath, fsmonitor, ssh/editor/pager, shell aliases, submodule/remote urls, insteadOf, credential helper). Complements the env-disabling in #123.

bricks.head: 7795d2723796a950a1ebd32db716fd51dfe76eec
bricks.summary: The conductor: driveEffect(ops) owns the canonical crash-recoverable ORDER over an injected EffectOps bundle. Proven over a double: the one committed path + every halt/quarantine branch.

bricks.head: 24d4827b36418ad80b8387391f6bd721ac3901b4
bricks.summary: Crash-at-every-phase recovery table: planRecovery(phase, consumed) chooses the safe action under two laws (never blindly re-execute; never create a second candidate). Exhaustive over all 15 phases x the consume marker.

bricks.head: f010437a3c9a336ca708948b43643f09318ccf56
bricks.summary: Docs-only integration guide: mission requirement -> brick, the driveEffect composition order + recovery table, proven-vs-remaining, and the seven held invariants.

invariantsHeld: Git begins only after consuming a durable PREPARED effect.
After a crash in EXECUTING, observe reality before acting; never blindly re-execute.
No clean success carries a null completion receipt.
No crash creates a second candidate.
Post-effect settlement is a projection only — no authority, signature, key, or content crosses into the store.
Every ambiguous outcome is quarantined, never silently accepted.
Nothing in this lane signs, pushes, merges, or mutates main; nothing grants authority.

remaining.brick: Concrete EffectOps wiring — supply advance/reconcile (state machine), runHermeticRehearsal, snapshotProtected/verifyIsolation, validateSettlement + Sam 2 store, EffectAuditLedger, and the hardened localCandidateStage.materializeCandidate; delegate the live candidate-stage ceremony to driveEffect.
remaining.gatedOn: The base bump after the comprehensive integration #135 (and R52 #120) merge to main. Not startable until the primitives are co-resident on main with Sam 2's durable store.
remaining.noNewAuthorityPath: The owner gate and the kernel reference monitor remain the sole authorities.

blocker: The admin-merge gate. #120 (R52) and #135 (comprehensive overnight, 11 bricks) are both green on Node 20 + 22 all night; main was never merged (Codex admin-merge required; Sam 1 is push-only). The base never advanced from 5ae15481, so no lane could complete end-to-end wiring.
attributionNote: In Sam 1's integration ledger, heads 4c591486 (effect-settlement #129) and 68aa9e85 (effect-audit #131) are Sam 3, not Sam 4 — both are in #135 correctly; flagged for an accurate per-lane record.
